title: Is every post on this sub a scam?
author: FineTough3648
selftext: I see many many posts of people posting their successe. Every comment seems to be warning that the post is a scam. is anyone actually making money using ai trading? How do we tell the scams from the not so scams?

comments.author: FilmFreak1082
comments.body:
Well, mine - getioi.app - is for sure not a scam. And i try to make it clear through extensive transparency on the site... + my app works exclusively on binance.com, yiu don't have to tranfer funds anywhere, you don't have to give the bot any permission to withdraw funds and you can test if with dry run bots to see how it performs before connecting your account... and account connection just allowy the bot to trade for you buy & sell-> nothing else. + also works with subaccounts so you can try real trading with minimal 100$ -> that's how even my brother tested it before jumping to 1000+ budget. 🤷‍♂️
I am also available on the discord for questions.

replies.author: FilmFreak1082
replies.body: Its an Android app that connects to your Binance account via API and trades automatically. Spot only, no leverage. You pick a pair, set a budget, press start. You can test with dry run bots (fake money, real algorithm) before risking anything. [getioi.app](http://getioi.app) if you want to look.

replies.author: FilmFreak1082
replies.body: No. Different marketplace, different prices, fewer (much fewer) trading pairs... need to buidl a coinbase version for US. 🤷‍♂️

replies.author: Quirky-Bit-6813
replies.body: Do you have a high watermark policy ?

replies.author: FilmFreak1082
replies.body: No high watermark - two pricing options. Commission model: 0.065% per trade on buy and sell. The exit target covers the commission so every completed trade is net positive for you. Or flat monthly subscription which cuts all commissions entirely - you trade commission-free. Your choice depending on what you prefer.

replies.author: Quirky-Bit-6813
replies.body: Then how do I ensure that your bot will not make false or fake trades to earn commissions? There is exactly an app like that. I will not reveal names. But it profits at the expense of users and will not protect profits.

replies.author: FilmFreak1082
replies.body:
you ensure it by testing your bot settings with a dry run (simulation) bot - you see how that performs. everything is very transparently reported in the analytics screen (even for simulation bots - they include everything)  
if you don't like the result, change the min exit condition.  
OR just simply pay for the subscription (prices are reasonable even for  bad market returns   - currently between 10-15% per month) -> then NO commission on any trades. DONE. Doesn't matter as long as it's making a profit. :)

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body:
reddit.com/r/ai_trading/comments/1sx57io/experimenting_with_llmbased_trading_via_alpaca/

My post isn't a scam. I'm trading and making modest gains, started in January, am on my second trading bot. Getting downvoted for no reason though. Probably so the scams can surface here. Looking for likeminded people. There's definitely something here and I'm down to share what I know and hear people out if anyone has any insights. We're on the cusp of this working I believe. The models are smart enough, just need the architecture that works.

comments.author: EfeBeAh
comments.body: Pretty much. Except for mine. I will make you one million in 12 months not guaranteed. You could totally go broke. DM for details

replies.author: theRealDamnpenguins
replies.body: Well - at least you're honest about being dishonest ;)

comments.author: wadap12345
comments.body: Yeah, same for every single income/money related sub lol

comments.author: Fragrant-Priority702
comments.body: This is totally going to make a verdict more obvious. @remindmebot

comments.author: RadiantBox466
comments.body: rough heuristic ive been using: if the post shows a pnl screenshot without a trade log, its a cherry pick. if it shows a trade log without a drawdown chart, still a cherry pick. anyone actually running something real is usually more interested in talking about what broke than what worked

comments.author: Yinnebecivil
comments.body:
Not every post is of that direction. You have to do your due diligence to find out the answers. Some people because of past experience shout ‘scam’ without even verifying this and as soon as an individual see these comments they automatically assume that is the truth. 

Yes there is individuals/groups out there that live of tricking people however there are systems out there that have a public track record and have the data to prove otherwise. 

Negative news travels faster than positive news 🗞️

replies.author: Curious_accountant_
replies.body: Does these people have legit websites that is able to show when search it on google is it secretive that doesn’t even show on google and just hidden and only u see it when u talk with the people who want u to get rich

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body:
Hi Curious, I sort of understood your question/statement.. my answer would be yes, there are sites that are not hidden to show real verified information. For example, there is a publicly accessible site called MyFxbook where an individual or business can link their trading account there platform which would then showcase all the details for the account. 

This would present the performance analytics of the said account, if it’s a live or demo account, win rate, percentages running from average monthly returns, daily, track record features etc. everything is presented so you can’t hide behind that

replies.author: Curious_accountant_
replies.body: Can I link bot trading page there too

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body:
Yes you can! This client account is running on my automated system. I’ll share MyFxbook so you can see how it interacts!

[QUANT FX ALGO](https://www.myfxbook.com/members/fxenvy/fluxforex-quant-algo--e2/11948627)

replies.author: Curious_accountant_
replies.body: If I can’t the broker in the fxbook then it’s must be a scam right does the broker name mean their website link url right I don’t much about it just got into it and got curious because the one I got into was Trexomarkets

comments.author: UnitedAcanthaceae118
comments.body:
Do NOT spend money on anything mentioned. And more importantly do NOT deposit money to any site mentioned here. Very high chance you'll get scammed.

The best way to go about AI trading is to simply go to [Everstrike.io](https://everstrike.io) and write a prompt. Then run the prompt with free credits in demo mode. If it performs well (unlikely) then go from there. If it doesn't, then abandon the idea and go do something else with your time.

comments.author: StevenVinyl
comments.body: no, not every post is a scam

comments.author: meshifthenelse
comments.body: There's always a lot of fluff in every sub.
